skill.name: debug-tauri
skill.description: Guides local Tauri WebView debugging with screenshot capture, console log analysis, process checks, and structured debug reports.
skill.summary: Debug Tauri WebView apps with local screenshots, logs, process checks, and report templates.
skill.author: 8beeeaaat

security_audit.summary: The static findings are mostly documentation examples, local shell helpers, and expected filesystem paths for Tauri debugging. I found no evidence of prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or screenshot upload, but the skill intentionally captures screens, inspects processes, and reads local logs. Publish with a warning because users should review local debug data before sharing it.

medium_findings.title: Local Screenshot Capture Can Expose User Data
medium_findings.description: The skill intentionally captures Tauri windows or the screen for debugging. This is legitimate, but screenshots can include private app data if shared outside the local workflow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The screenshot behavior is explicit in both documentation and executable scripts. I found no upload path, so the risk is local data exposure rather than confirmed exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Local Debug Scripts Inspect Processes And Logs
medium_findings.description: The bundled scripts run process checks, resource inspection, grep analysis, and log file reads. These actions are expected for debugging but expose local runtime details.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are concrete and local, and the inputs are mostly quoted. The behavior is legitimate for a debugger but should be disclosed to users.

medium_findings.title: Application Logs May Contain Sensitive Data
medium_findings.description: The skill guides users to read persistent Tauri logs and analyze recent errors. Logs can contain tokens, personal data, or internal URLs depending on the target app.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads are intentional and local. The sensitivity depends on the target application logs, so the risk is moderate rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Trigger Static Flags
low_findings.description: Many external command findings are markdown examples for Tauri setup, log reading, or migration. They are not hidden execution paths inside the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced examples or user-facing instructions. They do not automatically execute when the skill is loaded.

low_findings.title: Network Findings Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URL findings point to Tauri documentation or repository links. I found no code path that sends collected logs or screenshots to a remote endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in reference sections. No evidence found of network transmission in the reviewed scripts or skill instructions.

low_findings.title: Relative Plan Link Looks Like Path Traversal But Is Inert
low_findings.description: A migration reference links to a relative .claude plan path. This is a documentation artifact and not executable filesystem traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path appears as a markdown link. It does not read files, execute commands, or request credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: Screen Capture Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes screenshot APIs and a legacy macOS screencapture helper. Screenshots should be treated as sensitive debug artifacts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The screen capture behavior is directly documented and implemented. The pattern is dangerous only if users capture or share sensitive screens.

dangerous_patterns.title: Process Enumeration
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper scripts enumerate running app processes and print process details for debugging.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are direct and visible. They are expected debugger behavior and do not show stealth or persistence.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Log File Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill reads Tauri log files from standard platform-specific locations and can summarize log contents.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The access pattern is intentional and local. The risk comes from possible sensitive content in application logs.

content.value_statement: Tauri WebView issues often require screenshots, logs, process checks, and state details from different places. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured local workflow for collecting and analyzing that evidence.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether a named Tauri app process is running.
Captures screenshots through plugin guidance and a legacy macOS helper.
Finds and summarizes Tauri log files across supported platforms.
Counts errors, warnings, and common issue patterns in logs.
Guides WebView state collection for URL, title, user agent, and viewport.
Provides a structured debug report template for findings and fixes.
limitations: Requires the target app to include the needed Tauri debug plugins.
Legacy screenshot scripting is macOS-only and deprecated.
Cannot diagnose issues if logs are unavailable or not initialized.
Collected screenshots and logs may need manual redaction before sharing.

use_cases.title: Diagnose WebView failures
use_cases.description: Collect screenshots, process details, and logs when a desktop WebView renders incorrectly or fails during development.

use_cases.title: Trace runtime errors
use_cases.description: Review console output and WebView state to connect visible UI problems with JavaScript errors.

use_cases.title: Validate debug readiness
use_cases.description: Check whether logging, screenshot capture, permissions, and expected files are available before reproducing a bug.

prompt_templates.title: Check app status
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to check whether my Tauri app is running. My app binary name is [name]. Report the process status and next step.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze recent logs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to analyze Tauri logs for bundle ID [bundle id]. Summarize errors, warnings, and likely causes.

prompt_templates.title: Capture visual evidence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to guide screenshot capture for my running Tauri app. Identify visible UI problems and connect them to logs.

prompt_templates.title: Run full investigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to run the full Tauri debug workflow. Validate setup, inspect logs, capture visual evidence, and produce a structured report.

output_examples.input: Check my Tauri app named atlas and tell me why the window is blank.
output: Visual evidence: the main window is blank after startup.
Log summary: recent errors mention a failed frontend asset load.
Likely cause: the app cannot resolve the expected WebView entry path.
Next step: verify build output paths and rerun the log check after restart.

output_examples.input: Analyze logs for bundle ID com.example.notes.
output: Log summary: errors and warnings were counted from the expected platform log path.
Primary pattern: repeated network failures appear before the UI error.
Secondary pattern: no WebGPU errors were detected.
Recommendation: fix the failed request first, then rerun the same log analysis.

output_examples.input: Validate whether the debug tooling is ready before QA starts.
output: Setup status: process checks, log discovery, and screenshot checks were reviewed.
Readiness issue: plugin logs are missing or empty.
Recommended action: initialize logging at app startup and reproduce the issue once.
QA note: redact screenshots and logs before sharing them outside the team.

best_practices: Run setup validation before collecting screenshots or logs.
Prefer official Tauri plugins over deprecated platform-specific scripts.
Review screenshots and logs for secrets before sharing reports.
anti_patterns: Running screenshot capture on unrelated desktop content.
Sharing raw logs without checking for tokens or personal data.
Treating one screenshot as proof without matching it to logs.

faq.question: What does this skill debug?
faq.answer: It supports local debugging of Tauri WebView apps, especially UI, logging, screenshot, process, and setup issues.

faq.question: Does it upload screenshots or logs?
faq.answer: No evidence found of upload behavior. The reviewed scripts and documentation use local files and local analysis.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What setup is required?
faq.answer: The target app should include the relevant Tauri debug, logging, and screenshot plugins with permissions configured.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill handles screenshots, process details, and logs. These are legitimate but can contain sensitive local information.

faq.question: Can it fix bugs automatically?
faq.answer: It can propose fixes from collected evidence, but implementation still depends on the target application code.
